title: Multi-Clock SDC Setup
prompt: Define two clocks: a 500 MHz core clock on port clk_core and a 200 MHz IO clock on port clk_io, and mark them asynchronous to each other.
expected_output: Two named clocks with correct periods; cross-clock paths are not timed.
starter_code:
# Write your SDC here
# clk_core = 500 MHz (2 ns), clk_io = 200 MHz (5 ns)
solution_code:
create_clock -name clk_core -period 2.0 [get_ports clk_core]
create_clock -name clk_io   -period 5.0 [get_ports clk_io]
set_clock_groups -asynchronous \
  -group {clk_core} -group {clk_io}

title: False Path and Multicycle Path
prompt: Constrain a reset net to be a false path and allow a slow datapath reg2reg to take 2 cycles.
constraints: Use set_false_path for rst_n and set_multicycle_path 2 (setup) with the matching hold exception.
expected_output: Reset is not timed; the multicycle path is checked over 2 cycles.
solution_code:
set_false_path -from [get_ports rst_n]
set_multicycle_path 2 -setup -from [get_pins src_reg/Q] -to [get_pins dst_reg/D]
set_multicycle_path 1 -hold  -from [get_pins src_reg/Q] -to [get_pins dst_reg/D]

title: Generated (Divided) Clock
prompt: A flop divides clk by 2 to produce clk_div at its Q pin. Define the generated clock for STA.
constraints: Use create_generated_clock -divide_by 2 referencing the master clock clk.
expected_output: STA sees clk_div as clk/2 derived from clk.
starter_code:
# Master clock clk already defined on port clk
# Define the generated clock on div_reg/Q

title: Input/Output Delay Budget
prompt: Set input delay 1.5 ns and output delay 1.2 ns on all data ports relative to clk, and a max transition of 0.3 ns.
expected_output: IO timing budgets and a transition design rule are applied.
starter_code:
# clk already created with create_clock
# Write the IO and DRC constraints
solution_code:
set_input_delay  -clock clk 1.5 [remove_from_collection [all_inputs] [get_ports clk]]
set_output_delay -clock clk 1.2 [all_outputs]
set_max_transition 0.3 [current_design]

title: Clock Uncertainty (Skew + Jitter)
prompt: Apply 0.15 ns setup uncertainty and 0.05 ns hold uncertainty to model skew and jitter on clk.
expected_output: Available period is reduced for setup and increased margin required for hold.
starter_code:
# clk already defined
# Write the uncertainty constraints

title: Two-Flop Synchronizer
prompt: Synchronize an asynchronous 1-bit signal into the clk domain using two flip-flops.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, async_in. Output: sync_out.
expected_output: sync_out is async_in delayed by two clk cycles, metastability-hardened.

title: Divisible-by-3 FSM
prompt: Read a serial bit stream MSB-first and assert div3 when the number seen so far is divisible by 3.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, in. Output: div3. Track remainder mod 3.

title: Population Count (Ones Counter)
prompt: Count the number of 1s in an 8-bit input.
constraints: Module name: top. Input: data[7:0]. Output: count[3:0].

title: Find First Set (Lowest Set Bit Index)
prompt: Return the index of the lowest set bit of an 8-bit input, with a valid flag.
constraints: Module name: top. Input: data[7:0]. Outputs: idx[2:0], valid.
expected_output: idx points to the LSB-most 1; valid=0 if data==0.

title: Gray Code Counter
prompt: Build a 4-bit counter that outputs values in Gray-code order.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n. Output: gray[3:0].
expected_output: Successive outputs differ by exactly one bit.

title: Switch Debouncer
prompt: Debounce a noisy button: output stays stable and only updates after the input is steady for N samples.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, noisy. Output: clean. Use a saturating counter (N=4).
expected_output: clean follows noisy only after 4 consecutive equal samples.

title: Glitch-Free Clock Multiplexer
prompt: Select between two clocks clk0 and clk1 without producing glitches on clk_out.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk0, clk1, rst_n, sel. Output: clk_out. Use the standard gated-handshake mux.
expected_output: Output switches cleanly only when the target clock is low.

title: Clock Gating Cell (Latch-Based)
prompt: Implement an integrated clock-gating cell: gate clk with enable, using a low-active latch to avoid glitches.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, en. Output: gclk. Latch en on the low phase of clk.
expected_output: gclk pulses only when enable was sampled high; no partial clock pulses.

title: Pulse Synchronizer (Toggle Handshake)
prompt: Pass a single-cycle pulse from a fast source clock to a slower destination clock safely.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: sclk, dclk, rst_n, pulse_in. Output: pulse_out. Use toggle + 2-flop sync + edge detect.
expected_output: Each src pulse produces exactly one dst pulse.

title: Low-Power Counter with Enable
prompt: Build an 8-bit counter that only toggles flops when count_en is high (clock-enable style, saves power).
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, count_en. Output: cnt[7:0].
expected_output: cnt holds when count_en=0, increments when count_en=1.

title: Valid-Ready Skid Buffer
prompt: Implement a 1-deep skid buffer that registers a valid/ready stream without dropping data when downstream stalls.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, in_valid, in_data[7:0], out_ready. Outputs: in_ready, out_valid, out_data[7:0].
expected_output: No data loss; in_ready deasserts when the buffer is full and downstream is stalled.

title: One-Hot to Binary Encoder
prompt: Convert an 8-bit one-hot input to a 3-bit binary index.
constraints: Module name: top. Input: onehot[7:0] (exactly one bit set). Output: bin[2:0].
expected_output: bin = index of the set bit.

title: Pipelined 8-bit Adder (2 stages)
prompt: Add two 8-bit numbers with one pipeline register so the sum appears one cycle later.
constraints: Module name: top. Inputs: clk, rst_n, a[7:0], b[7:0]. Output: sum[8:0] (registered).
expected_output: sum = a + b, delayed by one clock.

title: Fixed-Priority Arbiter (4 requests)
prompt: Grant the lowest-index active request among four; lower index has higher priority.
constraints: Module name: top. Input: req[3:0]. Output: gnt[3:0] (one-hot, at most one set).
expected_output: gnt selects the lowest-index requesting bit.
